---
title: "The Unreasonable Syntactic Expressivity of RNNs"
description: "John Hewitt at EMNLP 2020 on encoding 10^20-state DFAs in 150-unit LSTMs."
author: "synesis"
date: "2020-10-20"
categories: [NLP, paper, conference, deep learning, links]
image: cover.jpg
---

![The Unreasonable Syntactic Expressivity of RNNs. Image from [John Hewitt](https://nlp.stanford.edu/~johnhew/rnns-hierarchy.html).](cover.jpg){fig-align="center"}

How an LSTM can encode a DFA with 10^20 states with only 150 hidden units (and seems to be able to learn it too).

"The Unreasonable Syntactic Expressivity of RNNs", by John Hewitt: <https://lnkd.in/gqtByfu>


*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_emnlp2020-activity-6724216766861602816-0TYr).*
